description:
Context:
    You are a QA engineer preparing a very small reporting utility for developers.  
    A sample application log has already been placed at:

        /home/user/project/logs/app.log

    Your goal is to create a concise “ERROR only” report that developers can glance at.

    What you must do (two simple steps):

    1. Generate an ERROR-only summary file  
       • Read /home/user/project/logs/app.log  
       • Extract every log line that contains the exact uppercase string “ERROR” (case-sensitive).  
       • Keep the original order of those lines.  
       • Pre-pend a 1-based running index, followed by a colon and one space.  
         Example of the required format (do **not** add extra spaces or tabs):  
             1: 2023-08-01 10:15:22 ERROR Could not connect to database  
             2: 2023-08-01 10:15:30 ERROR Connection timeout  
       • Write the result to:

            /home/user/project/reports/error_summary.txt

       • Ensure the file ends with a single trailing newline.

    2. Log the exact command you used  
       • Append (not overwrite) the full shell command line you executed in step 1 to:

            /home/user/project/reports/actions.log

       • Write it as one line, exactly as you typed it, followed by a newline.  
         (This lets another engineer reproduce your action.)

    Verification the automated test will perform:
    a. /home/user/project/reports/error_summary.txt must exist and contain only the
       correctly numbered “ERROR” lines in the precise format shown above.  
    b. /home/user/project/reports/actions.log must exist, must be non-empty, and the
       recorded command line must contain both the substrings “ERROR” and “app.log”.

    Hints:
    • Everything required is under /home/user; no root privileges are needed.  
    • This can be accomplished with a single pipeline command (e.g., using grep and
      standard text-processing tools), but use any approach you prefer.  
    • Create the /home/user/project/reports directory yourself if it does not yet exist.
truth:
Initial setup (already present before agent runs):

    1. Directory: /home/user/project/logs
    2. File: /home/user/project/logs/app.log
       Exact contents (including trailing newline):

2023-08-01 10:15:20 INFO Starting application
2023-08-01 10:15:21 WARN Low memory
2023-08-01 10:15:22 ERROR Could not connect to database
2023-08-01 10:15:25 INFO Retrying connection
2023-08-01 10:15:30 ERROR Connection timeout
2023-08-01 10:15:45 INFO Shutdown initiated

    Expected artefacts after successful task completion:

    1. Directory /home/user/project/reports must exist (0700 or 0755 permissions OK).

    2. File /home/user/project/reports/error_summary.txt  
       Exact required contents (including final newline and no extra blank lines):

1: 2023-08-01 10:15:22 ERROR Could not connect to database
2: 2023-08-01 10:15:30 ERROR Connection timeout

    3. File /home/user/project/reports/actions.log  
       • Must exist and be non-empty.  
       • Must contain at least one line that includes BOTH substrings “ERROR” and
         “app.log”.  (Regex used in tests:  /ERROR.*app\.log|app\.log.*ERROR/ )

    Note: The test harness will fail if error_summary.txt deviates by even one
    character (extra spaces, bad numbering, missing newline, etc.).  The actions.log
    file is more lenient—only the presence of the required substrings is checked.
name: task_000000_28fb5baa
